label: Filter Array
description: Filter an array by a typed field predicate. Replaces the JS-expression-only path through `transform` for the common 'keep rows where X' case.

settings.name: items
settings.label: Items (template or upstream)
settings.description: Path-template to the array. Omit to use the upstream connection's input directly.

settings.name: field
settings.label: Field (dot-path)
settings.description: Dot-path on each item to read for the predicate (e.g. 'metadata.name', 'severity', 'status.phase').

settings.name: operator
settings.label: Operator
settings.description: Predicate operator. 'exists' ignores the value field. 'in'/'not_in' require the value to be an array. 'matches_regex' compiles value as a regex pattern.

settings.name: value
settings.label: Value
settings.description: Comparand for the operator. Omit for 'exists'. Use a JSON array literal for 'in'/'not_in'.

ai.shortDescription: Filter an array by a field predicate (eq/neq/gt/contains/exists/etc.).
ai.whenToUse: When you need to keep only the rows of an upstream array that satisfy a single typed predicate — without writing JS in transform.
ai.examplePrompt: filter the pods to only those where status.phase != 'Running'
ai.promptHints: Output: {{steps.X.filtered}} (array), {{steps.X.droppedCount}}, {{steps.X.totalCount}}. Use 'contains' for substring match on strings or membership on arrays.

outputAssertions.name: shape_complete
outputAssertions.expression: result && Array.isArray(result.filtered) && typeof result.droppedCount === 'number' && typeof result.totalCount === 'number'
outputAssertions.errorMessage: filter_data output is missing required keys (filtered, droppedCount, totalCount).
